meta.description: Instructions for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers using the C# SDK Triggers on: **/*.cs, **/*.csproj

original_definition.skill_description: Instructions for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers using the C# SDK Triggers on: **/*.cs, **/*.csproj

actions.name: Use the ModelContextProtocol NuGet package (prerelease) for
actions.description: Use the ModelContextProtocol NuGet package (prerelease) for most projects: `dotnet add package ModelContextProtocol --prerelease`

actions.name: For stdio transport, use `WithStdioServerTransport()` when b
actions.description: For stdio transport, use `WithStdioServerTransport()` when building the server

actions.name: Use `WithToolsFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register
actions.description: Use `WithToolsFromAssembly()` to auto-discover and register all tools from the current assembly

actions.name: Test MCP servers using the `McpClient` from the same SDK or
actions.description: Test MCP servers using the `McpClient` from the same SDK or any compliant MCP client

actions.name: [McpServerTool, Description("Analyzes content using the clie
actions.description: [McpServerTool, Description("Analyzes content using the client's LLM")]

rules.description: Always configure logging to stderr using `LogToStandardErrorThreshold = LogLevel.Trace` to avoid interfering with stdio transport
Always include comprehensive descriptions for tools and parameters to help LLMs understand their purpose

directives.description: Use ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore for HTTP-based MCP servers
Use ModelContextProtocol.Core for minimal dependencies (client-only or low-level server APIs)
Use the `[McpServerToolType]` attribute on classes containing MCP tools
Use the `[McpServerTool]` attribute on methods to expose them as tools
Use the `[Description]` attribute from `System.ComponentModel` to document tools and parameters
Support dependency injection in tool methods - inject `McpServer`, `HttpClient`, or other services as parameters
Use `McpServer.AsSamplingChatClient()` to make sampling requests back to the client from within tools
Expose prompts using `[McpServerPromptType]` on classes and `[McpServerPrompt]` on methods
Tool methods can be synchronous or async (return `Task` or `Task<T>`)
Use `CancellationToken` parameters in async tools for proper cancellation support
